pages.full_text:
Villa Ephrussi de Rothschild.
Saint-Jean-Cap-Ferrat.

She laid out the grounds into attractive garden
"rooms" - Japanese, Spanish, Stone, Provençal,
French, Florentine, Sèvre, Exotic, Rose. There
is a lovely "tempetto" + water course down to
formal parterres (illusion of distance in spacing of
water courses.
pages.notes: Page contains a large colour postcard/photo of the Villa Ephrussi de Rothschild with caption written vertically alongside it. Text below describes the gardens. 'Sèvre' could be 'Sèvres' (a garden style named after the porcelain). 'tempetto' likely 'tempietto'. No handwritten page number visible.

pages.full_text:
The interior of the house has absolutely
gorgeous Fragonard & Boucher decorations, chinese
clothes & shoes, painted wood, wood carvings, stone
carvings, tapestries, carpets. Marvellous porcelain.
She only lived here for 4 years after 1912, because
then her husband died. She willed it to France (to
be added to by furniture from all her other villas
after her
death)

Madame Ephrussi's bedroom
pages.notes: Handwritten page number '65' in top-right corner. Page contains a colour photo of an ornate bedroom interior with caption 'Madame Ephrussi's bedroom' written vertically alongside it. Text continues from left page describing the Villa Ephrussi de Rothschild. 'chinese' is lower-case as written. The phrase 'all her other villas' — 'villas' is partially cut by the photo but readable in context.
